fact: November 29 is exactly 33 days before New Year's Day - that's the same number of vertebrae in your spine!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is when many trees have completely given up on their leaves and stand totally naked against the sky.
Polar bears in the Arctic are getting seriously chunky right now, packing on blubber before the sea ice forms for their hunting season.
The sun sets before 5 PM in most of the northern United States on this date - that's earlier than most kids' dinner time!
November 29 falls smack in the middle of Sagittarius season, when people born under this sign are supposedly extra adventurous and can't sit still.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, jacaranda trees are exploding with bright purple flowers that carpet the ground like nature's confetti.
This date sits exactly one month before the winter solstice in the north, making it like the countdown timer to the year's shortest day.
Reindeer in northern regions are growing their winter coats so thick right now that they look like walking fur balls with legs.
The morning frost in cold places creates ice crystals that look like tiny glass needles poking up from the ground.
November 29 is when the Geminids meteor shower starts building up - space rocks are already beginning their light show rehearsal!
In many northern cities, this is when streetlights start turning on before kids even get out of school because darkness comes so early.
Squirrels are in full panic mode right now, frantically burying nuts like tiny furry doomsday preppers.
